text: I built a role-based leave management system for the HyScaler assignment using React for the frontend, Laravel for the API layer, and PostgreSQL for persistence. The workflow is intentionally simple and interview-friendly: employees submit leave requests, managers review them, and approved requests update leave balances.
Core rule: leave balances change only after manager approval, never when the employee first submits a request.

rows: Login, dashboards, request forms, and manager review UI
Users, leave requests, and leave balances

text: The frontend stores a bearer token locally and restores the session on refresh.
The React UI shows different dashboards for employees and managers based on the user's role.
The Laravel service layer owns approval logic so controllers stay thin and easy to explain.
A shared leave-day utility keeps the day-counting rule consistent across the app.

rows: Bootstraps auth state and routes users into the correct dashboard
Leave application flow and personal request history
Login, session hydrate, and logout endpoints
Leave request listing, submission, and review endpoints
PostgreSQL schema for the three core tables

text: The shared leave-policy utility was executed locally and its date-range checks passed.
The Laravel and React source was written to be runnable on a normal machine with Composer and npm installed.
Seeded demo accounts are included for employee and manager login.
Employee: employee@example.com / password. Manager: manager@example.com / password.
